sourceMovie.title: Focus
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Focus (2015) stuck because it commits to its romance premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Glenn Ficarra's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bride Wars
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Focus clicked for you, Bride Wars (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Focus?
faq.answer: Try Bride Wars, The Hating Game, The Good Girl—each shares DNA with Focus while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Focus?
faq.answer: Focus (2015) is best described as Romance, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Focus worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
